mapping.a_element: tadpole correction to the local expansion rate (as a proposed indicator of decaying effective cosmological constant Λ)
mapping.b_element: excess energy from spontaneous graviton creation due to imbalance in virtual graviton processes

mapping.a_element: gauge invariant operator measuring renormalized curvature scalar smeared over a window function
mapping.b_element: self-consistent one-loop quantum gravity equations with BBGKY hierarchy yielding exact acceleration solutions

mapping.a_element: infrared gravitons in de Sitter space
perturbative analysis of secular screening (or lack thereof)
mapping.b_element: quantum fluctuations of geometry leading to macroscopic acceleration

brief_justification: While both papers address quantum gravitational effects on cosmological expansion, Paper A focuses on gauge invariance and the absence of secular screening in a perturbative framework, whereas Paper B relies on a non-perturbative BBGKY hierarchy and self-consistent one-loop quantum gravity to derive acceleration from virtual graviton imbalance. The core mechanisms (gauge-invariant renormalization vs. self-consistent graviton creation dynamics) differ fundamentally.
